description: Permissioned Domain Commands - Create, update, and delete permissioned domains that gate access via credentials on XRPL

userStories.title: permissioned-domain create command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to create a permissioned domain with a set of accepted credentials so that I can define which credential holders are permitted in my domain.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/commands/permissioned-domain.ts with permissionedDomainCommand registered in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts
`xrpl permissioned-domain create --credential <issuer>:<type>` submits PermissionedDomainSet tx with no DomainID field (creates new domain)
--credential is repeatable (collect array); 1–10 credentials accepted; error if 0 or >10 provided
<type> in --credential <issuer>:<type> is treated as UTF-8 and auto-encoded to hex via convertStringToHex from xrpl
`--credentials-json <json>` accepts JSON array of {"issuer":"r...","credential_type":"hex..."} objects as alternative input
--credential and --credentials-json are mutually exclusive; error if both provided
On success, outputs `Domain ID: <hash>` extracted from CreatedNode where LedgerEntryType === 'PermissionedDomain' in tx metadata
`--json` outputs {"result":"success","domainId":"<hash>","tx":"<hash>"}
`--dry-run` prints unsigned tx JSON and exits without submitting
`--no-wait` submits without waiting for validation
Supports --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password key material

Validation tests (no network): missing credentials exits with error, >10 credentials exits with error, both --credential and --credentials-json exits with error, invalid issuer address format exits with error
E2E tests on testnet: create with 1 credential, create with 3 credentials, --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Requires PermissionedDomains and Credentials amendments. AcceptedCredentials wire format uses Issuer (address) and CredentialType (hex). If testnet does not have the amendment, use devnet instead and note this in a comment.

userStories.title: permissioned-domain update command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to update the accepted credentials of an existing permissioned domain I own so that I can change which credentials grant access.
acceptanceCriteria: `xrpl permissioned-domain update --domain-id <hash> --credential <issuer>:<type>` submits PermissionedDomainSet tx with DomainID field set
--domain-id required; must be 64-char hex string; error if missing or malformed
Same --credential / --credentials-json rules as create (1–10, mutually exclusive)
Help text clearly states update replaces the entire credentials list (not a merge)
On success, outputs `Domain ID: <hash>` and `Tx: <hash>`
`--json`, `--dry-run`, `--no-wait` supported
Supports --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Validation tests (no network): missing --domain-id exits with error, invalid --domain-id format exits with error, missing credentials exits with error
E2E tests on testnet: create domain in beforeAll, update credential list, verify via account_objects, --json, --dry-run
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: PermissionedDomainSet with DomainID = update; without DomainID = create. The AcceptedCredentials array fully replaces the existing list on update.

userStories.title: permissioned-domain delete command
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to delete a permissioned domain I own so that I can remove it from the ledger and reclaim the reserve.
acceptanceCriteria: `xrpl permissioned-domain delete --domain-id <hash>` submits PermissionedDomainDelete tx
--domain-id required; must be 64-char hex string; error if missing or malformed
On success, outputs `Deleted domain: <hash>` and `Tx: <hash>`
`--json` outputs {"result":"success","domainId":"<hash>","tx":"<hash>"}
`--dry-run`, `--no-wait` supported
Supports --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password
Validation tests (no network): missing --domain-id exits with error, invalid --domain-id format exits with error
E2E tests on testnet: create domain in beforeAll then delete it, verify gone via account_objects (type: 'permissioned_domain'), --json, --dry-run, --no-wait
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Error conditions: tecNO_ENTRY if domain doesn't exist, tecNO_PERMISSION if not the owner. Use isDeletedNode from xrpl to confirm deletion in metadata if needed.
